data.title: BMO Financial Non-GAAP EPS of C$3.28 beats by C$0.25, revenue of C$9.34B beats by C$320M
data.description:
• BMO Financial press release (BMO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$3.28 beats by C$0.25.
  • Revenue of C$9.34B (+4.2% Y/Y) beats by C$320M.
  • Provision for credit losses (PCL) of C$755 million, compared with C$1,523 million
  • Reported return on equity (ROE) of 10.7%, compared with 11.4%; adjusted ROE1 of 11.8%, compared with 7.4%
  • Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) Ratio3 of 13.3%, compared with 13.6%
  • Declared a quarterly dividend of C$1.67 per common share, an increase of C$0.04 or 2% from the prior quarter, and an increase of C$0.08 or 5% from the prior year.

data.title: BMO Financial declares CAD 1.67 dividend
data.description:
• BMO Financial (BMO) declares CAD 1.67/share quarterly dividend, 2.5% increase from prior dividend of CAD 1.63.
  • Forward yield 3.78%
  • Payable Feb. 26; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • See BMO Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Donaldson tops Q1 estimates; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Donaldson press release (DCI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.94 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $935.4M (+3.9% Y/Y) beats by $12.46M.

  • Updated Fiscal 2026 Outlook

    Adjusted full-year EPS is expected to be within a range of $3.95 and $4.11 vs. consensus of $4.01, an increase from the previous range of $3.92 and $4.08 and excluding a $0.03 per share net gain recognized in the first quarter 2026. Fiscal 2025 GAAP and adjusted EPS were $3.05 and $3.68, respectively. Sales are forecast to grow 1% to 5% year over year, consistent with prior expectations and inclusive of a pricing benefit of approximately one percentage points. Currency translation and tariff impacts are projected to be immaterial.

    Mobile sales are projected to be flat to up 4% versus prior year, in line with previous guidance. Off-Road sales are forecast to increase mid-single digits as certain market conditions begin to improve following declines in the prior year. On-Road sales are now expected to be flat due to muted global truck production. Aftermarket sales are forecast to grow low-single digits, driven by continued market share gains and increasing vehicle utilization rates.

    Industrial sales continue to be forecast to increase between 2% and 6% compared with 2025. IFS sales are projected to grow mid-single digits with growth across all businesses, including dust collection and Power Generation. Aerospace and Defense sales are expected to be flat compared with prior year after cycling against all-time-high levels in the prior year.

    Life Sciences sales are expected to grow between 1% and 5% versus 2025, consistent with prior guidance and driven by ongoing strength in Food & Beverage and Disk Drive.

    Adjusted 2026 operating margin is expected to be between 16.2% and 16.8%, up from previous guidance of between 16.1% and 16.7%. Fiscal 2025 operating margin was 13.4%, or 15.7% on an adjusted basis. The year-over-year improvement in operating margin is driven by gross margin expansion and operating expense leverage.

    Interest expense is estimated to be approximately $24 million and other income is forecast to be between $14 million and $18 million. Donaldson projects a fiscal 2026 adjusted effective income tax rate of between 23.5% and 25.5%.

    Capital expenditures are forecast to be between $65 million and $85 million and adjusted free cash flow conversion is expected to be in a range of 85% and 95%. For the full year, Donaldson anticipates repurchasing between 2.0% and 3.0% of its shares outstanding.

data.description:

data.title: WeRide draws backing from Cathie Wood’s ARKQ fund
data.description:
China’s autonomous driving company WeRide (WRD) has recently drawn a series of investments from Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest.

According to public disclosures, ARK purchased 858,295 WeRide ADSs in the past three trading sessions, including 191,149 shares on December 3 via its ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ).

The move fits ARK’s strategy of backing disruptive technologies. ARK’s Big Ideas 2025 report projects that autonomous mobility could become a multi-trillion-dollar market, with global robotaxi fleets potentially expanding to tens of millions of vehicles by 2030.

WeRide, which holds autonomous driving permits across eight countries, continues to attract high-profile investors. Recent filings show major shareholders such as Bosch, Fidelity, Greenwoods, Hudson Bay, Invesco, M&G, Mirae Asset, Morgan Stanley Investment Management, Nvidia, Temasek, and several long-only and sovereign wealth funds from Asia and the Middle East.

WRD ADRs were up +1.6% premarket on Thursday.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Canadian Imperial Bank raises dividend by 10% to CAD 1.07
data.description:
• Canadian Imperial Bank (CM) declares CAD 1.07/share quarterly dividend, 10.3% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.97.
  • Payable Jan. 28; for shareholders of record Dec. 29; ex-div Dec. 29.
  • See CM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Meta starts deactivating kids' Facebook, Instagram accounts in Australia
data.description:
Meta's (META) Facebook and Instagram have begun deactivating accounts of children under the age of 16 in Australia, a week before the country's unprecedented teen social media ban will take effect.

The apps are also blocking the creation of new accounts for under-16 users in Australia. Around 150,000 Facebook accounts and 350,000 Instagram accounts are expected to be impacted.

Julie Inman Grant, commissioner of Australia's internet regulator eSafety, was initially concerned about the "blunt-force" approach on the issue, pointing to incremental regulatory changes over the past nine years.

"But I think we've reached a tipping point, where something more forceful needed to be done," Grant said at the Sydney Dialogue summit.

She referred to the ban as "the first domino," which is why social media companies previously pushed back on the move. To note, Malaysia is planning a similar ban from next year.

Other apps that must comply with Australia's ban are Snapchat (SNAP), TikTok (TIKTOK), Twitch, X, YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL), Kick and Reddit (RDDT). Non-compliance could lead to fines of up to A$49.5M (~$33M).

data.title: Nutex Health extends share buyback program through March 2026
data.description:
• Nutex Health (NUTX) on Thursday said that its Board of Directors has approved an extension of the previously authorized stock repurchase program.
  • The program, originally announced on August 14, authorized the repurchase of up to $25.0 million of the company's common stock over a six-month period, commencing upon the filing of the company's Form 10-Q for the quarter ended June 30, 2025.
  • The board has extended the program through March 31, 2026.

data.title: Canadian Imperial Bank beats Q4 estimates
data.description:
• Canadian Imperial Bank press release (CM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$2.21 beats by C$0.13.
  • Revenue of C$7.58B (+14.5% Y/Y) beats by C$320M.

data.title: Palantir teams up with Nvidia, CenterPoint Energy to speed AI infrastructure buildout
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) has partnered with Nvidia (NVDA) and CenterPoint Energy (CNP) to launch Chain Reaction, a software platform designed to address a key constraint on AI development.

“The bottleneck to AI innovation is no longer algorithms; it is power and compute. Chain Reaction is designed to address this directly by accelerating the AI buildout with energy producers, power distributors, data centers and infrastructure builders to transform aging power generation into high-uptime resources capable of meeting AI’s massive demand,” the company said.

CenterPoint Energy (CNP) is adopting Chain Reaction to speed up access to power and improve operational visibility across ITS critical assets, while Nvidia (NVDA) plans to accelerate its AI infrastructure installations across the U.S.

“The energy infrastructure buildout is the industrial challenge of our generation,” said Tristan Gruska, Palantir’s Head of Energy and Infrastructure. “But the software that the sector relies on was not built for this moment. We have spent years quietly deploying systems that keep power plants running and grids reliable. Chain Reaction is the result of building from the ground up for the demands of AI.”

Source: Press Release
